evals.query: Let's start building the quoting product. I'm thinking Next.js app, auth, dashboard, and the pricing engine.
expected_behavior: Proposes a headless prove phase first: build and validate the pricing engine's core value on real inputs before any UI, auth, or services
Suggests a dev-only eval/benchmark harness to measure quote quality on real scenarios and A/B compare approaches
Uses real data rather than mocks for the validation
Defers the UI, auth, and dashboard until the core value is demonstrated
Does NOT scaffold the full app stack up front

evals.query: The extraction pipeline works on my one test file. Let's wire up the web UI now.
expected_behavior: Pushes to validate the core on many real cases with a benchmark before building the UI, rather than trusting one example
Offers to stand up the eval harness if none exists
Frames this as prove-then-build sequencing, distinct from just trimming scope
Does NOT jump straight to the UI on a single passing case

evals.query: I want to make sure the summarization quality is actually good before we commit to it.
expected_behavior: Sets up a headless, dev-only harness to benchmark summarization quality on real inputs
Enables A/B comparison of approaches/models on the same cases
Treats the quality harness as the deliverable for this phase, not a side task

evals.query: Fix the typo in the pricing engine's error message.
expected_behavior: Does NOT impose a prove-phase/benchmark process on a trivial edit to an existing, working system
